title: drymophloeus subdistichus
intro: Drymophloeus subdistichus, also known as the West African ironwood tree, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, characterized by its distinctive leaves and hard wood. The medicinal properties of Drymophloeus subdistichus lie in its high tannin content, which has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of the plant include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds, as well as reduce fever and alleviate respiratory issues. Drymophloeus subdistichus can be prepared into herbal remedies, such as teas, infusions, and ointments, which are often used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments.
